problem.summary: Compute LayerNorm and RMSNorm backward gradients with explicit row reductions and parameter-gradient reductions, using PyTorch, Transformer Engine, and Triton as library/framework baselines.
constraints: Template-only ROCm parity port; CUDA-origin timing records were not copied and no ROCm profiler-counter evidence is claimed
Support LayerNorm and RMSNorm contracts separately
Record whether saved statistics come from forward or are recomputed
Accumulate dgamma/dbeta in fp32 and document reduction strategy

verification.method: The HIP harness compares dx, dgamma, and dbeta against a deterministic CPU reference using deterministic inputs and saved-statistic reuse. PyTorch and Triton baseline runners are provided for future ROCm timing, but no ROCm result artifacts are attached yet.

curation.notes: ROCm parity port from CUDA task `normalization-backward`. This is a template/harness scaffold only; CUDA-origin result artifacts were intentionally not copied, and no ROCm timing, correctness, or profiler-counter evidence is claimed yet. Run on AMD hardware, attach ROCm version, hipcc flags, gfx target, and label HIP-event-only results as timing-only.
